name: Pu'u o Mahuka Heiau
tagline: Oahu's largest ancient Hawaiian temple — a sacred 17th-century site on a bluff overlooking Waimea Bay with breathtaking views.
parking: Small gravel lot at the end of Pupukea Rd
bestTime: Sunrise or sunset for the most powerful atmosphere
talkStory: Oahu's largest ancient Hawaiian temple covers nearly two acres on a bluff overlooking Waimea Bay. Built in the 17th century, this National Historic Landmark was a place of human sacrifice and spiritual power. Today, locals still leave offerings of ti leaves wrapped around stones. The views of Waimea Valley and the ocean are breathtaking. Most tourists visit Waimea Bay below without knowing this sacred site exists just up the road. Treat the site with deep respect.
